_comment: MATCHING EXERCISE (type: match) — learner taps a word on the left, then its English equivalent on the right. Good for introducing a set of 3–5 related words together. 244 in the corpus.
_wid: Always null for match exercises. Proficiency is tracked per word via the wids array inside each pair, not at the exercise level.
prompt: Match each nature word to its meaning
_prompt: Describe what the learner is matching. Usually 'Match each [category] to its meaning'.
sub: Tap a word on the left, then its match on the right
_sub: This exact string is standard for all match exercises. Required.
_pairs: 3–5 pairs. Each pair: target = Kanien'kéha word, english = English meaning, wids = array of glossary IDs (usually one, occasionally two for compound concepts). The right column is shuffled automatically — do not try to pre-shuffle.
cm: Io'nikonhraién:ta'ne! These four words form the core of land vocabulary. Notice how -shón:'a appears in both ohontsa'shón:'a (grass) and ohneka'shón:'a (water) — it is a diminutive suffix meaning 'small' or 'little ones of'.
_cm: Correct message shown after all pairs are matched. Use it to highlight a pattern or connection across the set. Required.
wm: That answer is incorrect — please try again.
_wm: Shown when a pair is matched incorrectly. Required.
